installation_technical.fact: A typical residential solar install follows these steps: site assessment, shade analysis, system design, permit application, utility interconnection application, installation, inspection, utility interconnection approval, system activation.
NEC Article 690 governs solar PV systems. Key requirements: rapid shutdown (690.12), disconnecting means, ground fault protection, arc fault protection, wire sizing per 690.8.
DC wire sizing: multiply max system voltage by 1.25 for continuous load. Use USE-2 or PV wire for DC runs exposed to sunlight.
Rapid shutdown required for all rooftop systems. Enphase microinverters comply automatically (module-level shutdown). String inverters require a rapid shutdown device.
Roof penetrations must be flashed and sealed. Use Quickmount PV or IronRidge flashings. Never use caulk alone on roof penetrations.
Ground mount systems require trenching for DC/AC conduit runs. Conduit must be buried minimum 24 inches (rigid metal) or 18 inches (PVC schedule 80).
String inverter systems: all panels in a string must have same orientation and tilt. One shaded panel reduces output of entire string.
Microinverter systems (Enphase): each panel has its own inverter, shade on one panel does not affect others. Better for complex roofs.
System grounding: equipment grounding conductor (EGC) required for all metallic racking and equipment. Array grounding per NEC 690.47.
AC disconnect required within sight of utility meter. Many utilities require a lockable AC disconnect outside the home.
Optimal panel tilt in North Texas: 28-32 degrees (matches latitude). South-facing arrays produce maximum annual output.
North Texas average peak sun hours: 5.0-5.5 hours/day. Used to calculate system output: kW system x peak sun hours = daily kWh.

equipment_specs.fact: Enphase IQ8 microinverter: grid-agnostic, 366W AC output, 97.5% CEC efficiency, 25-year warranty, supports Sunlight Backup mode without battery.
Enphase IQ Battery 5P: 5kWh usable, 3.84kW continuous output, 10-year warranty, indoor/outdoor rated, pairs with IQ8 microinverters.
Tesla Powerwall 3: 13.5kWh usable, 11.5kW peak output, integrated inverter (no separate solar inverter needed), 10-year warranty.
SolarEdge H1 inverter with optimizers: panel-level optimization, 99% weighted efficiency, 12-year inverter warranty, 25-year optimizer warranty.
Common panel brands: Qcells (Q.PEAK DUO, 400-430W), REC Alpha (430W), Jinko Tiger Neo (440W), Canadian Solar (420W). All tier-1 manufacturers.
TOPCon panels (Jinko Neo, REC Alpha) have lower temperature coefficient (~0.30%/C) vs standard PERC (~0.35%/C), better performance in Texas heat.
Panel warranties: 25-year product warranty + 25-year performance warranty (80% output at year 25) standard for tier-1 panels.
IronRidge XR100 and XR1000 rails are most common racking. Good for shingle, metal, and tile roofs. Pair with IronRidge flashings.
Enphase Envoy/IQ Gateway required for microinverter systems — communicates with all IQ microinverters and sends data to Enlighten monitoring platform.

utility_policy.fact: CoServ Electric is a member-owned electric co-op serving most of Wise County TX including Decatur, Bridgeport, Boyd, Chico, Rhome, Newark, Aurora.
CoServ net metering: residential solar customers receive retail rate credit for excess generation exported to grid. Submit Interconnection Application before installation.
CoServ interconnection process: submit application → engineering review (2-4 weeks) → approval → install → final inspection → permission to operate (PTO).
Oncor serves Parker County TX (Weatherford, Aledo, Willow Park). Oncor is the TDU (transmission/distribution utility) — retail choice is available (choose your REP).
Oncor net metering: excess solar exported to grid credited at avoided cost rate (lower than retail). Some REPs offer better buyback rates — shop accordingly.
ERCOT (Electric Reliability Council of Texas) manages the grid for 90% of Texas including all N-Tech service counties. Deregulated market.
Texas Property Tax Code Section 11.27: solar energy devices are exempt from property tax assessment. Adding solar does not increase your property tax bill in Texas.

permits_and_codes.fact: Wise County / City of Decatur: building permit required for solar installations. Submit structural drawings, electrical single-line diagram, and equipment specs.
Parker County / City of Weatherford: building permit required. Inspections: rough electrical and final. Must pass before utility interconnection.
Typical permit documents required: site plan, roof plan showing panel layout, structural calculations (or engineered letter), electrical single-line diagram, equipment spec sheets.
Rural properties in unincorporated county areas may not require a building permit but still require utility interconnection approval from CoServ or Oncor.
HOA rules: Texas law (SB 1626) prohibits HOAs from banning solar panels outright, but they can regulate placement to minimize street visibility.

market_and_pricing.fact: N-Tech Energy Solutions starting price: .40/watt. A 10kW system = 4,000 before 30% ITC = 6,800 net cost.
National average solar price: .80-.20/watt installed. N-Tech at .40/watt is significantly below market average.
North Texas average payback period: 8-12 years depending on bill size, system size, and utility rates. System lifespan 25-30 years.
Average residential system size in Texas: 8-12kW. A home using 1,500 kWh/month typically needs a 10-12kW system in North Texas.
Rule of thumb system sizing: monthly kWh usage / (peak sun hours x 30 days) = system kW needed. Example: 1500 kWh / (5.2 x 30) = 9.6kW.
Battery storage adds ,000-5,000 to system cost depending on capacity. Enphase IQ Battery 5P ~,000 installed, Tesla Powerwall 3 ~2,000 installed.

incentives.fact: Federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC): 30% of total system cost deducted from federal income tax. Available through 2032. Applies to panels, inverters, battery, and installation labor.
ITC is a tax credit, not a refund. You must have sufficient federal tax liability to use it. Can carry forward unused credit to future tax years.
Battery storage qualifies for 30% ITC if charged primarily from solar (90%+ solar charged). Standalone battery without solar qualifies at 30% as well under IRA 2022.
Texas has no state solar tax credit. However, Texas property tax exemption (Section 11.27) and sales tax exemption on solar equipment provide additional savings.
Texas sales tax exemption: solar energy devices are exempt from state sales tax under Texas Tax Code Section 151.317.

competitors.fact: Sunrun: largest US residential solar company. Uses leases and PPAs heavily. Customer complaints about long-term contract issues and customer service.
Tesla Solar: competitive pricing but slow installation timelines and limited local support. Uses Tesla Powerwall exclusively for storage.
Freedom Forever: national installer active in Texas. Uses subcontractors heavily — quality can vary by market.
Key N-Tech differentiator vs nationals: local company, owner-operated, no subcontractors, direct customer relationship, faster permitting turnaround.

local_area.fact: Wise County TX population: ~75,000. Growing bedroom community north of Fort Worth. Many rural and semi-rural properties ideal for solar.
Decatur TX is the Wise County seat. Fastest growing city in the county. Mix of new subdivisions and rural properties.
Parker County TX population: ~160,000. Weatherford is county seat. Rapidly growing western suburb of Fort Worth with high solar potential.
North Texas average electricity rate: 12-15 cents/kWh. CoServ rates slightly higher than average due to co-op structure.
North Texas solar resource: 5.0-5.5 peak sun hours/day average. One of the best solar resources in the continental US.

general_solar.fact: Solar panel output degrades ~0.5% per year. After 25 years a panel producing 400W will produce approximately 350W (87.5% of original).
Shade analysis tools: Solargraf, Aurora Solar, HelioScope. These use satellite imagery and 3D modeling to calculate shading impact on output.
String inverter vs microinverter: microinverters cost 20-30% more but perform better with shading, complex roofs, and provide panel-level monitoring.
Cleaning solar panels: light rain keeps most panels clean. Manual cleaning with soft brush and water 1-2x per year maximizes output in dusty areas like North Texas.

troubleshooting.fact: If a microinverter goes offline: check Enphase Enlighten app. Likely cause: communication issue, grid voltage out of range, or hardware fault. Replace under warranty if hardware fault.
System producing less than expected: check for shading (new tree growth, debris), inverter faults, grid outage, or soiled panels.
Ground fault on string inverter: indicates insulation damage in DC wiring. Disconnect system and inspect all DC cable runs. Do not operate until resolved.
